channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal as 'unacceptable'
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday branded Iran's terms for ending the Middle East war "totally unacceptable," raising the likelihood of renewed conflict after weeks of negotiations. Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Suspected hantavirus case triggers emergency deployment to isolated island
item.description: As Americans are evacuated from the Dutch-flagged cruise ship linked to the hantavirus outbreak, two passengers have now tested positive. But in the South Atlantic, a scene from a Hollywood thriller: paratroopers dropping onto one of the world’s most remote inhabited islands with emergency medical supplies after a suspected case. A British national is now in a stable condition while isolating.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: PM Starmer clings on to power as Labour rivals make moves
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – May 11, 2026: First, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under pressure following the Labour Party's disastrous results in the local elections. Next, the Guardian features an exclusive extract from Iranian Nobel Prize winner Narges Mohammadi's diaries. Meanwhile, the situation in Cuba is on Libération's front page. Finally, Spanish sports papers celebrate Barcelona as the new La Liga champions.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak: US, French nationals test positive after evacuating ship
item.description: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. Following the positive test results, Spain defended the rigour of its sanitary measures during the complex evacuation Sunday of 94 people of 19 different nationalities from the MV Hondius, which is moored in the Canary Islands. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Bichat Hospital in central Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi released on bail for medical treatment
item.description: Iranian authorities on Sunday released Nobel peace prize winner Narges Mohammadi on bail following growing alarm over her health and she has already been transferred to Tehran for medical treatment, her supporters said. They had last week warned that Mohammadi, who won the 2023 prize in recognition of her decades of campaigning for human rights in Iran, was at risk of dying on prison after suffer two suspected heart attacks behind bars in Zanjan.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Russia, China and Iran: What do Beijing and Moscow want to gain from conflict?
item.description: Iran and the United States reached an impasse again Monday over how to end their war while their ceasefire grew increasingly shaky. As the situation in the Middle East keeps the world on edge, many regional observers say Iran’s resilience is thanks in part to the lifeline extended by two key allies: Russia and China. Both countries, at least publicly, say they seek de-escalation. But Western observers say they are providing crucial support to Teheran to help keep it in the fight. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French passenger from hantavirus ship tests positive
item.description: A French woman repatriated from a cruise ship struck by hantavirus has tested positive with the rare disease, France's health minister said Monday, in the country's first case since the outbreak. The woman, one of five French passengers flown back from the MV Hondius and placed in isolation in Paris, started to feel very unwell on Sunday night and "tests came back positive", Health Minister Stephanie Rist told the radio station France Inter. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Paris, France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus: Ship evacuations enter second day
item.description: The last 24 passengers aboard the MV Hondius are set to evacuate the ship on May 11, before the vessel gets disinfected and goes back to the Netherlands. At least two passengers have tested positive since disembarking.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Britain’s Starmer vows to prove ‘doubters’ wrong as he fights ouster calls
item.description: Embattled British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday vowed to prove his "doubters" wrong in a major speech aimed at quelling a mounting rebellion within his party after Labour suffered the worst local election results for a governing party in more than three decades last week. In a bid to restore "hope" to the country, Starmer pledged to put "Britain at the heart of Europe".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: REPLAY: Under-pressure UK PM addresses nation after election losses
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer sought to quell a mounting rebellion in his party on Monday by vowing to prove the doubters wrong and stay in power to avoid plunging ​Britain into a ‌new political crisis. A growing number of Labour lawmakers have turned on ⁠Starmer after his party suffered the worst local election results for a governing party in more than three decades last week, prompting a former junior ‌minister to threaten to seek a leadership contest if he fails to offer radical ⁠change.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Eurovision fever hits host city Vienna
item.description: Eurovision kicked off in Vienna on Sunday, with thousands of fans flocking to the city for events and music ahead of the song contest's grand final next weekend, with renewed calls for a boycott over Israel's participation. The Austrian capital is pulling out all the stops to host the 70th edition of the world's most-watched song contest, with many side events scheduled in the run-up to the final on May 16.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak renews painful memories for Patagonian village
item.description: Mailen Valle lost her father and two sisters during a hantavirus outbreak more than seven years ago in Epuyen, a village in Argentina's Patagonia region. With the recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship, hard memories have resurfaced for the 33-year-old. Tthe Epuyen outbreak recorded 34 cases and 11 fatalities between December 2018 and March 2019 in the town of 2,400 residents, situated in a part of the Andes where hantavirus is endemic.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Israel war on Iran: Hormuz island tourism takes a hit
item.description: More than two months into the US-Israel war on Iran, the continued disruption of the Strait of Hormuz deepens global economic strain. But beyond the shipping lanes, other industries are also taking a major hit. FRANCE 24's reporters travelled to Iran's Hormuz Island, where residents remain anchored amid the uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Macron in Kenya for summit showcasing Paris’s business ties with Africa
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron ​and more than 30 African leaders kicked off a summit in Kenya on Monday aimed at diversifying Paris' partnerships on the continent and clinching new investment deals. During a state visit on ​Sunday, Macron announced that French shipping group CMA CGM planned to invest 700 million ‌euros to modernise a terminal at the Kenyan port of Mombasa. FRANCE 24's Tom Canetti reports from Nairobi, Kenya.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Crude oil prices rise as Trump calls Iran's offer 'totally unacceptable'
item.description: Benchmark crude oil prices rose on Monday as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal to find an end to the war in the Middle East. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is leaving its mark on economies around the world, with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling for reduced fuel use and Chinese inflation gauges ticking up higher than expected. Also in this edition: French President Emmanuel Macron announces more than €1 billion in investment in Kenya.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: FRANCE 24 report: Over 17,000 US and allied troops take part in Balikatan drills
item.description: Over the past few weeks, US and Philippine forces have been engaged in their largest-ever joint military exercises. Known as Balikatan, the drills are taking place across the Philippines and are designed to boost regional security as Beijing grows increasingly assertive in the region. This year’s exercises have also featured French troops for the first time since Paris signed a Visiting Forces Agreement with Manila. FRANCE 24’s Jan Camenzind Broomby reports from the Philippines.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French evacuee from hantavirus-hit ship tests positive, health minister says
item.description: A French national evacuated from a luxury cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak tested positive for the virus, the French health minister said Monday. Four other French passengers from the ship tested negative, but will be re-tested, the minister added.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Watch live: Macron meets young entrepreneurs at Africa Forward summit
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron on Monday aims to promote France’s new approach to Africa at the inauguration of the Africa Forward Summit in Kenya, which is being held for the first time in an Anglophone country. The French leader is meeting 450 young people from across the continent and France, who are set to share their ideas for the future of the African continent. Watch FRANCE 24's live coverage of the event.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ligue 1: PSG close in on title as they defeat Brest at home
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain beat Brest (1-0) and are all but certain to be crowned French champions. With just two games remaining, PSG are six points clear of Lens and have a significantly better goal difference.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Former Thai PM Thaksin Shinawatra released from prison on parole
item.description: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra was released from prison on parole on Monday after serving eight months of a reduced sentence tied to corruption charges. His return to public life marks another chapter in a political saga that has dominated and deeply divided Thailand for nearly two decades.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France’s Macron opens Africa summit in Kenya with push for new partnership
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron began a visit to Kenya on Sunday ahead of the Africa Forward Summit, where he will promote France’s new approach to Africa based on partnership rather than influence. The gathering comes amid France’s military withdrawal from West Africa and renewed debate over its post-colonial role on the continent.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Iran war cannot end before uranium is ‘taken out’, Netanyahu says
item.description: Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the US-Israeli war can be considered over, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told a US broadcaster after President Donald Trump said Tehran's latest proposal for ending the conflict was "totally unacceptable". Iran on Monday said its terms were "legitimate" and "generous". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump calls Iran's response to US peace proposal 'totally unacceptable'
item.description: Iran rejected the latest US peace proposal on Sunday, prompting a swift dismissal from President Donald Trump, who called the response “TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!” on social media. The exchange marks another setback in efforts to ease tensions in the Persian Gulf, where disrupted shipping and rising energy prices continue to fuel global concern.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
